fields.description:
## Context
Give an `identity`'s public keys a LIFECYCLE with cryptographic continuity:
a trust-on-first-use (TOFU) genesis key, and subsequent key add/rotate/revoke
changes that must each be signed by a currently-valid key of that identity (fills
git-bug's unimplemented `IsProtected`). A key's validity is scoped to its ERA —
its position in the authoritative MERGED event log — never to an attacker-set
event timestamp. Builds on ddbe (identity + `keys`) and bde5 (authorship
sign/verify via `rebar.authorship.v1`).

## Why
Without a signed rotation chain, anyone who can append a KEY event to an identity
could inject their own key and forge authorship. Anchoring validity to merged-log
position (not wall-clock) stops an attacker from backdating a forged event into a
revoked key's window.

## What
### 1. KEY lifecycle events on the identity (TAG_DELTA precedent)
Add key-management event types folded by the reducer into an era-scoped keyring:
- Event types (new constants in `src/rebar/reducer/_version.py`, added to the
  known-event-type set; bump the reducer version so older clones
  preserve-and-ignore — see the TAG_DELTA v3 note): `KEY_ADD` and `KEY_REVOKE`
  (a rotate = a `KEY_REVOKE` of the old key + a `KEY_ADD` of the new, or a single
  `KEY_ADD` that supersedes; keep the two primitive events, model rotate as their
  composition).
- `KEY_ADD` data: `{public_key: <authorized-keys line>, signature: <DSSE envelope
  or null for genesis>}`. `KEY_REVOKE` data: `{public_key: <line>, signature:
  <DSSE envelope>}`.
- New reducer processor `process_key_event` (in `_processors.py`, dispatched from
  `_replay.py`) folds these into `state["keyring"]`: a list of
  `{public_key, added_era, revoked_era|null}` records, where `era` is the ordinal
  position (see §2). `state["keys"]` (from ddbe) becomes the DERIVED set of keys
  whose validity window contains the CURRENT (latest) era — i.e. currently-valid
  keys — so existing readers keep working.

### 2. Era = merged-log ordinal (NOT event timestamp)
The reducer already replays events in the canonical order `sorted({HLC-ts}-{uuid}`
filename prefix`)` — identical on every clone after sync (see reducer/_cache.py,
_processors.py:538). Define an event's ERA as its 0-based INDEX in that canonical
replay of the identity's event stream. This is the authoritative "merged-log
position": it is fixed at merge time and consistent across clones, and it is NOT
the attacker-controllable `data`-level timestamp. A key added at era `a` and
revoked at era `r` is VALID for signed events whose era is in `[a, r)` (open at
`r`: a key cannot sign at or after its own revocation). A never-revoked key has
`revoked_era = None` (valid for all eras >= `a`).

### 3. Write-time refusal of unsigned/invalidly-signed KEY changes
A `KEY_ADD`/`KEY_REVOKE` after genesis is REFUSED (the write raises, no event is
appended) unless its `signature` is a valid `rebar.authorship.v1` authorship
envelope (bde5 `verify_authorship`) over the KEY event's canonical content,
produced by a key that is CURRENTLY valid for this identity (valid at the era
immediately preceding this change). The GENESIS `KEY_ADD` (the first key event for
an identity that has no keys yet) is TOFU: accepted WITHOUT a signature (nothing to
chain to). Surface this via a new command/library seam
`add_identity_key` / `revoke_identity_key` (CLI `rebar identity key add/revoke`)
that performs the signature check before appending.

### 4. Era-scoped authorship verification (extends bde5)
Add `verify_authorship_at_era(envelope, identity_id, era, *, repo_root=None) ->
Verdict`: resolve the identity's keyring, build the trust root from ONLY the keys
whose `[added_era, revoked_era)` window contains `era`, and verify. An event whose
signing key was revoked before the event's era fails; one from within the key's
valid era passes. (`verify_authorship` from bde5 stays as the "current-era"
convenience.)

## Scope
In: the KEY_ADD/KEY_REVOKE events + reducer keyring fold + era computation, the
signed-rotation write gate (add/revoke seam), TOFU genesis, and
`verify_authorship_at_era`. Out: auto-signing every normal event / the merge-gate
enforcement (3183), Jira mapping (264f/2f13). Key material lives in `keys` as
before; private keys are operator-held (signatures are produced out-of-band via the
bde5 signing seam).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A `KEY_ADD`/`KEY_REVOKE` on an identity that already has a genesis key is
  REFUSED unless accompanied by a valid authorship signature from a currently-valid
  key of that identity; a test asserts an UNSIGNED key-add is refused (raises / no
  event appended) while the genesis key-add (first key) is accepted TOFU.
- [ ] An event whose authorship envelope was produced by a key REVOKED at an
  earlier era fails `verify_authorship_at_era` for that event's era, while an event
  signed within the key's valid era PASSES (test builds a keyring with an add then
  a revoke and checks both sides of the boundary).
- [ ] Validity is computed from merged-log ordinal position (the reducer's
  canonical replay order), NOT from the event's `data` timestamp: a test that sets
  a misleading/backdated `data` timestamp on an event does NOT change which era
  (and therefore which key set) governs it.

## file_impact
- `src/rebar/reducer/_version.py` (KEY_ADD/KEY_REVOKE constants + version bump + known-type set)
- `src/rebar/reducer/_processors.py` (process_key_event: fold keyring + era + derive current keys)
- `src/rebar/reducer/_replay.py` (dispatch KEY events)
- `src/rebar/attest/authorship.py` (verify_authorship_at_era + era-scoped trust root)
- `src/rebar/_commands/identity.py` (add_identity_key/revoke_identity_key seam with the signed-rotation write gate; CLI wiring)
